divergence: the clock was never invented — appointments are 'morning-ish', trains leave when full, and 'on time' cannot be translated
story_angle: solemn obituary for a beloved institution nobody can remember the purpose of
headline: National Schedule Standardization Agency Laid to Rest Between 'Before Lunch-ish' and 'After Snack-ish'
deck: Agency that once dictated precise appointment slots now memorialized for its enduring dedication to delightful ambiguity.
article:
The National Schedule Standardization Agency (NSSA) has closed after a state funeral timed for 'morning-ish'. Mourners drifted in at their own pace and filled the hall to its unregulated limit.

Staff who remained could not recall the agency's founding purpose. The last session ended when people simply left; nearly half the old records now read only 'after lunch-ish'.

The Pentagon issued a late note praising the agency's 'reliable vagueness' and warning that snack breaks might now drift without coordination. Residents thanked the NSSA for protecting the habit of delay, while others wondered how any group could stretch a fifty-minute meeting across two hours without ever saying why.
author: Margaret T. Chronister, Bureaucratic Historian

oped.title: Precision's Passing: My Homburg Shall Rule the Hours
oped.author: Sir Reginald Snodgrass, Minister of Unscheduled Affairs and Curator of the National Fedora Museum
oped.body: The NSSA's end frees sensible people from rigid clocks. Those who once demanded '9 o'clock sharp' wore hats that blocked life's useful drift. Trains should leave only when passengers have adjusted their headgear and finished a snack. All future meetings will be set by the angle of my homburg, which understands time better than any chart. Complaints from the hatless will be ignored; without clocks, order depends on proper millinery, not agency ledgers. My fedora collection now claims final say on every schedule.

classifieds.text: Startup founder urgently needs scheduler fluent in 'before lunch-ish' to replace our vanished agency contact. Must tolerate 4-hour arrival windows and coffee-fueled existential debates.
Liquidating NSS Agency relics: 47 unused appointment ledgers, a box of 'on time' rubber stamps, and one broken hourglass. First come, first served-ish.
Sorry I missed our 2025 meeting—I thought 'next week-ish' meant sometime before the next eclipse. Still free most afternoons that feel right.
Class-action notice: former clients suing over missed deadlines now seek collective compensation in the form of vague future favors. Join between now and then.

panels.caption: Gravedigger wipes sweat: 'Burial slotted between before lunch-ish and after snack-ish.'
Bureaucrat frowns at empty chairs: 'The standardization committee never confirmed the window, so we're still waiting.'
Coffin lid creaks open: 'Told you precision was dead on arrival.'

joke.setup: Funeral services for the National Schedule Standardization Agency were announced for between 'Before Lunch-ish' and 'After Snack-ish'.
joke.punchline: Zero attendees showed; everyone assumed the other guy would handle the vague window.
joke.text: Funeral services for the National Schedule Standardization Agency were announced for between 'Before Lunch-ish' and 'After Snack-ish'. Zero attendees showed; everyone assumed the other guy would handle the vague window.

sponsor_ads.headline: ChronoMush Wristband 4.0
sponsor_ads.body: Finally, a device that vibrates sometime after you wake up and sometime before you forget why you looked at it. Syncs seamlessly with the new national nothing o'clock.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Now with 87% fewer arguments about what 'ish' means.

sponsor_ads.headline: NSSA Memorial Interment
sponsor_ads.body: Join us for the agency's burial between Before Lunch-ish and After Snack-ish; no RSVPs accepted because they cannot be translated.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Catered by the same caterers who never showed up last time.

sponsor_ads.headline: FullTrain Ticketing
sponsor_ads.body: Departures occur the moment the carriage feels sufficiently crowded; arrive whenever the tracks decide they are done.

sponsor_ads.headline: VagueBook Personal Assistant
sponsor_ads.body: Books every appointment as 'sometime when we both remember' and cancels them when the sun changes its mind.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Free trial expires at an undisclosed future-ish.

stories.headline: Firms Eliminate Deadlines After Schedule Agency Dissolves
stories.deck: Project leads now track progress via hallway sightings instead of status calls
stories.article:
Corporations have struck every timed target from their handbooks since the NSSA closed. Teams now finish quarterly goals only when members meet at the coffee machine.

Midwest offices hold reviews solely when enough managers appear on the same afternoon, cutting disputes over missed targets by forty percent.

stories.headline: Daylight Thefts Rise as Patrol Windows Turn Indefinite
stories.deck: Suspects cite overlapping vague availability as their primary defense
stories.article:
Break-ins during former enforcement hours have risen twelve percent. Officers now answer calls after finishing other tasks or refueling, whichever comes first.

One repeat offender was cleared because officers reached the store only after the owner had returned, leaving no one to confirm timing.

real_world_echo: obliquely mirrors US nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia by eulogizing a vanished federal body that once coordinated precise bilateral timing windows now rendered meaningless
